title: Cross a Street Safely
summary: Cross a street at the right place and time, following traffic signals.
learnerPromise: You will be able to cross streets at the right place and time, using traffic signals and pedestrian crossings.
whyItMatters: Crossing streets safely reduces the risk of accidents and helps you move through your day with confidence. It is a basic skill that matters every time you are on foot near traffic.
realLifeUses: Crossing at a marked crosswalk with a walk signal
Finding a safe place to cross when there is no crosswalk nearby
Crossing with a group or helping a younger person cross safely
Paying attention to turning cars even when you have the right of way
materialsNeeded: A street with traffic signals or pedestrian crossings
Optional: a phone with a map to locate crosswalks
result: A safe street crossing at a proper location and time.
youWillLearn: How to use pedestrian signals and crosswalks
How to look both ways and check for turning vehicles
How to pick the safest crossing point when no crosswalk is nearby
How to stay alert to traffic even when you have the right of way
miniChallenge: On your next walk, cross at least two streets using crosswalks or safe crossing points. Make eye contact with drivers before stepping in front of cars.
steps: Stop at the curb or edge of the road and look for a crosswalk.
Check the traffic signal and press the pedestrian button if there is one.
Look left, right, and left again for cars, bikes, and buses.
Watch for turning vehicles even if you have a walk signal.
Cross straight and at a steady pace. Do not run or stop halfway.
completionCriteria: You crossed at a designated crosswalk or safe crossing point.
You checked both directions and for turning vehicles before crossing.
You completed the crossing calmly and without rushing.
commonProblems: Crossing while looking at a phone and not noticing traffic
Assuming a walk signal means all cars will stop
Darting between parked cars where drivers cannot see you
tips: Make eye contact with drivers when possible before crossing.
Remove earbuds or pause music when crossing busy streets.
Crosswalks with lights are safer than crossing mid-block.
If the intersection feels unsafe, wait for a gap in traffic or find a better crossing.
helpBoundary: If you are unsure whether a crossing is safe, look for a nearby crosswalk, pedestrian bridge, or traffic officer. Ask a trusted adult or local authority for guidance if a street feels too dangerous to cross.
